aliases: 凤梨 / 菠萝 (Fèng Lí / Bōluó)

chinese: 凤梨 / 菠萝 (Fèng Lí / Bōluó)

region_of_origin: Native to South America; major commercial producers include Malaysia, Thailand, and the Philippines. Malaysia (Johor, Sarawak) supplies Singapore markets. Morris and Josapine varieties dominate Singapore retail.
food_subgroup: Fresh ripe tropical fruit — skin and core removed, yellow flesh cut into chunks. Standard Singapore hawker cut-fruit serving: 1 cup chunks ≈ 165 g.

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code confirmed for raw pineapple fruit in available reference; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Singapore hawker cut-fruit serving is approximately one cup of pineapple chunks, weighing about 165 grams with skin and core removed.

nutrient_source.title: Pineapple, raw, all varieties — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 169124); cross-referenced with Health Promotion Board Singapore Foodo database
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (SR Legacy) + Health Promotion Board Singapore (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 169124 is raw pineapple, all varieties — an exact match for fresh ripe pineapple chunks. Values cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo for regional validation. All per-100g values are consistent between sources.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — Atkinson et al. 2008; University of Sydney GI Database entry for fresh pineapple
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Glycemic Index Foundation
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 59 (medium) for fresh pineapple is documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and consistent with Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI tables. GL per 165 g serving = (59 × 13.1 × 1.65) / 100 ≈ 12.8. This is a direct match for fresh raw pineapple; not an analogous estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (1 mg/100 g), moderate potassium (109 mg/100 g), high Vitamin C (47.8 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Fresh pineapple is highly suitable for hypertensive patients: its sodium content is negligible (1 mg/100 g), its potassium supports vascular tone, and its high Vitamin C (47.8 mg/100 g) contributes to endothelial health; one cup (165 g) per day is appropriate without restriction.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (~59), moderate glycemic load per serving (~12.8 per 165 g cup)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fresh pineapple has a medium GI of approximately 59 — not high-GI as commonly assumed — and a glycemic load of approximately 12.8 per one-cup (165 g) serving, making it acceptable for T2DM patients in controlled single-cup portions; pineapple juice should be avoided as it concentrates sugar and removes fibre and bromelain.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, high Vitamin C, bromelain anti-inflammatory activity
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Fresh pineapple contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, provides 47.8 mg/100 g Vitamin C (an antioxidant supporting vascular integrity), and contains bromelain — a proteolytic enzyme with observed anti-inflammatory and mild fibrinolytic properties in observational studies — making it a cardioprotective fruit choice when consumed fresh.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Bromelain (active only in fresh pineapple), manganese as MnSOD cofactor
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Fresh pineapple is beneficial for RA patients due to bromelain — a proteolytic enzyme that inhibits arachidonic acid metabolism and reduces prostaglandin synthesis — which is active only in fresh, uncooked pineapple (denatured above 60°C); canned or cooked pineapple provides no active bromelain.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pineapple (凤梨 / Nenas / அன்னாசி / अनानास / สับปะรด — Ananas comosus) is one of Singapore's most versatile and beloved tropical fruits. Sold pre-cut at hawker fruit stalls year-round, it is also a key ingredient in Nonya assam fish, pineapple fried rice, rojak, and Singapore's most iconic Chinese New Year snack — pineapple tarts (nastar). The flesh is vivid yellow, juicy, and intensely sweet-tart, with a distinctive tang from malic and citric acid. Nutritionally, fresh ripe pineapple is notable for three clinically relevant properties: (1) High Vitamin C — 47.8 mg per 100 g, supporting immune function and endothelial health; (2) Bromelain — a unique proteolytic enzyme complex found only in fresh pineapple (stem and flesh), with documented anti-inflammatory properties including inhibition of arachidonic acid metabolism and prostaglandin synthesis; bromelain is completely denatured above 60°C, so canned or cooked pineapple contains none; (3) Medium glycemic index of approximately 59 — contrary to the widespread assumption that pineapple is high-GI, it is medium-GI, and one cup (165 g) produces a glycemic load of approximately 12.8, which is acceptable for T2DM patients in controlled portions. The Morris and Josapine (sweet) varieties dominate the Singapore market, supplied primarily from Johor and Sarawak, Malaysia. Per 100 g: 50 kcal, 0.5 g protein, 0.1 g fat, 13.1 g carbohydrate, 9.9 g sugar, 1.4 g dietary fibre, 1 mg sodium. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 169124 cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo.
common_user_queries: สับปะรด โภชนาการ
meal_context: cooking ingredient (Nonya assam fish, pineapple fried rice, rojak)

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 169124 (raw pineapple, all varieties) — an exact match — cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo. GI of 59 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database for fresh pineapple. All values are consistent across both approved sources. Confidence is high.
notes: Saturated fat is confirmed 0.0 g per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 169124 — fresh pineapple contains no saturated fat by its natural composition (confirmed zero). Trans fat is confirmed 0.0 g — naturally zero for a fresh whole fruit. Cholesterol is confirmed 0.0 mg — naturally zero as this is a plant food. Vitamin B12 is confirmed 0.0 mcg — naturally zero as this is a plant food with no animal-derived components. Vitamin D is confirmed 0.0 mcg — naturally zero for fresh pineapple per USDA SR Legacy. Monounsaturated fat (0.01 g) and polyunsaturated fat (0.04 g) are present in trace amounts per USDA SR Legacy. Water content estimated at 86.0 g/100 g per USDA SR Legacy (86.0 g reported). Manganese is noted in the input LLM context as 0.93 mg/100 g (USDA SR Legacy confirms ~0.927 mg/100 g) — this is a notable nutrient for RA patients as a MnSOD cofactor; manganese is not a standard RESULT template field and is therefore documented here rather than in per_100g. Bromelain is a proteolytic enzyme active only in fresh pineapple; it is denatured above 60°C and absent in canned or cooked pineapple. GI of 59 is a direct University of Sydney GI Database value for fresh pineapple; GL per 165 g serving calculated as (59 × 13.1 × 1.65) / 100 = 12.76, rounded to 12.8. No -1 sentinel values remain in core fields; all per_100g fields are populated with confirmed numeric values from USDA SR Legacy.
